name: Navajun from Valdemadera — circular by the views to Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa
name_original: Navajun desde Valdemadera — circular por las vistas al Castillo de Magaña y Cigudosa
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Valdemadera traverse a diverse landscape characterized by scenic river valleys and varied terrain. The region offers a blend of flat sections and challenging ascents, set against a Mediterranean backdrop. Cyclists can expect routes with significant elevation gains, testing endurance across its hilly and mountainous areas. The area also features historical sites and charming villages, providing cultural points of interest along the rides. Best road cycling routes around Valdemadera The most popular road cycling route is The ascent San Felices from Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular by al Castillo de Magaña de Los, a 36.5 miles (58.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a challenging ascent with views towards Castillo de Magaña. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Southern valleys of the Iberian System in La Rioja, a difficult 81.7 miles (131.5 km) path. This long-distance route includes substantial elevation gain as it traverses the southern valleys of the Iberian System. Local road cyclists also love the Navajun from Valdemadera — circular by the views to Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa, a 35.9 miles (57.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views towards Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Valdemadera is defined by river valleys, challenging climbs, and historical sites. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdemadera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Valdemadera?
A: Road cycling routes in Valdemadera feature a diverse landscape, including scenic river valleys and varied terrain. You'll find a mix of flat sections for speed and challenging ascents that test endurance, set against a Mediterranean backdrop. The region is characterized by hilly and mountainous areas, with many routes offering significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Valdemadera?
A: While Valdemadera is known for its challenging routes, there are options for varying fitness levels. The guide includes 3 moderate routes, which are generally more accessible than the difficult ones. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 38.1 km ride with 459 meters of elevation gain, offering a good option for those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Valdemadera's routes often pass by or near significant historical sites and charming villages. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade&lt;/a&gt;, or visit picturesque towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama&lt;/a&gt;, which features castle ruins. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6373794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarnago&lt;/a&gt; with its Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6298738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Church&lt;/a&gt; also offers cultural excursions.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Valdemadera?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Valdemadera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383526284/the-ascent-san-felices-from-aguilar-del-rio-alhama-circular-by-al-castillo-de-magana-de-los" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The ascent San Felices from Aguilar del Río Alhama — circular by al Castillo de Magaña de Los&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383511516/navajun-from-valdemadera-circular-by-the-views-to-castillo-de-magana-and-cigudosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navajun from Valdemadera — circular by the views to Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa&lt;/a&gt;, both offering extensive loops through the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Valdemadera?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling in Valdemadera, offering milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the middle of the day, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, though cycling is often still possible.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Valdemadera?
A: While many routes in Valdemadera feature significant elevation gains and are rated moderate to difficult, families with older children or those comfortable with some climbing might enjoy the moderate options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate route that could be suitable for more experienced cycling families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Valdemadera?
A: Road cycling routes are generally not suitable for dogs, especially on paved roads with traffic. If you wish to bring your dog, it's recommended to look for dedicated cycling paths or consider other outdoor activities like hiking on trails where dogs are permitted and safe.

Q: Where can I find cafes or accommodation suitable for cyclists near Valdemadera?
A: The charming villages and towns along and near the cycling routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama&lt;/a&gt;, often provide amenities like cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These make for convenient stops to refuel or stay overnight during your cycling tour.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Valdemadera?
A: One of the longest and most challenging routes is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990763381/southern-valleys-of-the-iberian-system-in-la-rioja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southern valleys of the Iberian System in La Rioja&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 131.5 km (81.7 miles) and includes substantial elevation gain as it traverses the southern valleys of the Iberian System.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in Valdemadera?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes around Valdemadera featured in this guide, ranging from moderate to difficult in terms of challenge.

Q: What makes road cycling in Valdemadera unique?
A: Road cycling in Valdemadera is unique due to its combination of diverse terrain, including river valleys and challenging climbs, set within a Mediterranean landscape. The region also offers the opportunity to combine physical activity with cultural exploration, thanks to its historical sites and charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeperillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views, particularly towards historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383511516/navajun-from-valdemadera-circular-by-the-views-to-castillo-de-magana-and-cigudosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navajun from Valdemadera — circular&lt;/a&gt; route provides views towards Castillo de Magaña and Cigudosa. You can also find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Cornago from the GR 93&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4787693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Castle of Cervera from the GR 93&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Navajún is a municipality in Rioja Baixa. It is known worldwide for the pyrite mines, which are mined as ornamental minerals. The town's name possibly comes from the pre-Roman term nava, which in Basque means breadth.

Before reaching the town of Magaña, in one of the curves that descends to the Río Alhama, you can see the Castillo de Magaña and the whole of its town located at one of the meanders of the same river.

This small town in the Tierras Altas region is located in the northeast of the province, its term is bathed by the Alhama river, which in turn is a tributary of the Ebro. When you pass the road that comes from Castelruiz, you can see the town of cigudosa

waypoints.name: Views of the Castillo de Magaña
